sections.label: Section 2.460
sections.original: σὺ γὰρ ὤ , μόνα , ὦ φίλα γυναικῶν ,
sections.translation: For thou , and thou alone , most dear of women , hadst the courage to redeem thy husband from Hades in exchange for thy own life . Light lie the earth above thee , lady ! And if ever thy lord take to him a new wife , I vow he will earn my hatred

sections.label: Section 2.465
sections.original: στυγηθεὶς τέκνοις τε τοῖς σοῖς .
sections.translation: and thy children ’ s too .

sections.label: Section 2.466
sections.original: ματέρος οὐ θελούσας
sections.translation: His mother had no heart to plunge into the darkness of the tomb for her son , no ! nor his aged sire . [*] Their own child they had not the courage to rescue ,

sections.label: Section 2.470
sections.original: σχετλίω , πολιὰν ἔχοντε χαίταν .
sections.translation: the wretches ! albeit they were grey-headed . But thou in thy youth and beauty hast died for thy lord and gone thy way . O be it mine to have for partner such a loving wife ! for this lot is rare in life . Surely she should be my help-meet all my life

sections.label: Section 2.475
sections.original: δι ’ αἰῶνος ἂν ξυνείη .
sections.translation: and never cause one tear .

sections.label: Section 476
sections.original: ξένοι , Φεραίας τῆσδε κωμῆται χθονός ,
sections.translation: Mine hosts , dwellers on this Phersean soil ! say , shall I find Admetus in the house ?

sections.label: Section 478
sections.original: ἔστ ’ ἐν δόμοισι παῖς Φέρητος , Ἡράκλεις .
sections.translation: The son of Pheres is within , Heracles . Tell me what need is bringing thee to the Thessalian land ,

sections.label: Section 480
sections.original: πέμπει , Φεραῖον ἄστυ προσβῆναι τόδε .
sections.translation: to visit this city of the Pheraeans ?

sections.label: Section 481
sections.original: Τιρυνθίῳ πράσσω τιν ’ Εὐρυσθεῖ πόνον .
sections.translation: I am performing a labour for Tirynthian Eurystheus .

sections.label: Section 482
sections.original: καὶ ποῖ πορεύῃ ; τῷ προσέζευξαι πλάνῳ ;
sections.translation: And whither art thou journeying ? on what wandering art thou forced to go ?

sections.label: Section 483
sections.original: Θρῃκὸς τέτρωρον ἅρμα Διομήδους μέτα .
sections.translation: To fetch the chariot-steeds of Thracian Diomedes .

sections.label: Section 484
sections.original: πῶς οὖν δυνήσῃ ; μῶν ἄπειρος εἶ ξένου ;
sections.translation: How canst thou ? art a stranger to the ways of thy host ?

sections.label: Section 485
sections.original: ἄπειρος · οὔπω Βιστόνων ἦλθον χθόνα .
sections.translation: I am ; for never yet have I gone to the land of the Bistones .

sections.label: Section 486
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστιν ἵππων δεσπόσαι σ ’ ἄνευ μάχης .
sections.translation: Thou canst not master his horses without fighting .

sections.label: Section 487
sections.original: ἀλλ ’ οὐδ ’ ἀπειπεῖν μὴν πόνους οἷόν τ ’ ἐμοί .
sections.translation: Still I cannot refuse these labours .

sections.label: Section 488
sections.original: κτανὼν ἄρ ’ ἥξεις ἢ θανὼν αὐτοῦ μενεῖς .
sections.translation: Then shalt thou slay them and return , or thyself be slain and stay there .

sections.label: Section 489
sections.original: οὐ τόνδ ’ ἀγῶνα πρῶτον ἂν δράμοιμ ’ ἐγώ .
sections.translation: It will not be the first hard course that I have run .

sections.label: Section 490
sections.original: τί δ ’ ἂν κρατήσας δεσπότην πλέον λάβοις ;
sections.translation: And what will be thy gain , suppose thou master their lord ?

sections.label: Section 491
sections.original: πώλους ἀπάξω κοιράνῳ Τιρυνθίῳ .
sections.translation: The steeds will I drive away to the Tirynthian king .

sections.label: Section 492
sections.original: οὐκ εὐμαρὲς χαλινὸν ἐμβαλεῖν γνάθοις .
sections.translation: No easy task to bit their jaws .

sections.label: Section 493
sections.original: εἰ μή γε πῦρ πνέουσι μυκτήρων ἄπο .
sections.translation: Easy enough , unless their nostrils vomit fire .

sections.label: Section 494
sections.original: ἀλλ ’ ἄνδρας ἀρταμοῦσι λαιψηραῖς γνάθοις .
sections.translation: With ravening jaws they rend the limbs of men .

sections.label: Section 495
sections.original: θηρῶν ὀρείων χόρτον , οὐχ ἵππων , λέγεις .
sections.translation: Thou speakest of the food of mountain beasts , not of horses .

sections.label: Section 496
sections.original: φάτνας ἴδοις ἂν αἵμασιν πεφυρμένας .
sections.translation: Their mangers blood-bedabbled thou shalt see .

sections.label: Section 497
sections.original: τίνος δ ’ ὁ θρέψας παῖς πατρὸς κομπάζεται ;
sections.translation: Whose son doth he who feeds them boast to be ?

sections.label: Section 498
sections.original: Ἄρεος , ζαχρύσου Θρῃκίας πέλτης ἄναξ .
sections.translation: Ares ’ son , king of the golden targe of Thrace .

sections.label: Section 499
sections.original: καὶ τόνδε τοὐμοῦ δαίμονος πόνον λέγεις ·
sections.translation: This toil again is but a piece of my ill-luck ;

sections.label: Section 500
sections.original: σκληρὸς γὰρ αἰεὶ καὶ πρὸς αἶπος ἔρχεται ·
sections.translation: hard it ever is and still is growing steeper , if I with Ares ’ own-begotten sons must fight , first with Lycaon , next with Cycnus , while now I am bound on this third contest to engage the horses and their master .

sections.label: Section 505
sections.original: ἀλλ ’ οὔτις ἔστιν ὃς τὸν Ἀλκμήνης γόνον
sections.translation: Yet shall no man ever see Alcmena ’ s son trembling at his foemen ’ s prowess .

sections.label: Section 507
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ὅδ ’ αὐτὸς τῆσδε κοίρανος χθονὸς
sections.translation: See where Admetus , lord of this land , comes in person from the palace forth .

sections.label: Section 509
sections.original: χαῖρʼ , ὦ Διὸς παῖ Περσέως τ ’ ἀφ ’ αἵματος .
sections.translation: Hail ! son of Zeus , from Perseus sprung .

sections.label: Section 510
sections.original: Ἄδμητε , καὶ σὺ χαῖρε , Θεσσαλῶν ἄναξ .
sections.translation: Joy to thee also , Admetus , king of Thessaly .

sections.label: Section 511
sections.original: θέλοιμ ’ ἄν · εὔνουν δ ’ ὄντα σ ’ ἐξεπίσταμαι .
sections.translation: Would there were ! yet thy kindly heart I know full well .

sections.label: Section 512
sections.original: τί χρῆμα κουρᾷ τῇδε πενθίμῳ πρέπεις ;
sections.translation: Why dost thou appear with head shorn thus in mourning ?

sections.label: Section 513
sections.original: θάπτειν τιν ’ ἐν τῇδ ’ ἡμέρᾳ μέλλω νεκρόν .
sections.translation: To-day I am to bury one who is dead .

sections.label: Section 514
sections.original: ἀπ ’ οὖν τέκνων σῶν πημονὴν εἴργοι θεός .
sections.translation: Heaven avert calamity from thy children !

sections.label: Section 515
sections.original: ζῶσιν κατ ’ οἴκους παῖδες οὓς ἔφυσ ’ ἐγώ .
sections.translation: The children I have begotten are alive within my house .

sections.label: Section 516
sections.original: πατήρ γε μὴν ὡραῖος , εἴπερ οἴχεται .
sections.translation: Thy father maybe is gone ; well , he was ripe to go .

sections.label: Section 517
sections.original: κἀκεῖνος ἔστι χἡ τεκοῦσά μʼ , Ἡράκλεις .
sections.translation: No , Heracles , he lives ; my mother too .

sections.label: Section 518
sections.original: οὐ μὴν γυνή γ ’ ὄλωλεν Ἄλκηστις σέθεν ;
sections.translation: It cannot be thy wife is dead , thy Alcestis ?

sections.label: Section 519
sections.original: διπλοῦς ἐπ ’ αὐτῇ μῦθος ἔστι μοι λέγειν .
sections.translation: I can a twofold tale tell about her .

sections.label: Section 520
sections.original: πότερα θανούσης εἶπας ἢ ζώσης ἔτι ;
sections.translation: Dost mean that she is dead , or living still ?

sections.label: Section 521
sections.original: ἔστιν τε κοὐκέτ ’ ἔστιν , ἀλγύνει δ ’ ἐμέ .
sections.translation: She lives , yet lives no more ; that is my grief .

sections.label: Section 522
sections.original: οὐδέν τι μᾶλλον οἶδʼ · ἄσημα γὰρ λέγεις .
sections.translation: I am no wiser yet ; thy words are riddles to me .

sections.label: Section 523
sections.original: οὐκ οἶσθα μοίρας ἧς τυχεῖν αὐτὴν χρεών ;
sections.translation: Knowest thou not the doom she must undergo ?

sections.label: Section 524
sections.original: οἶδʼ , ἀντὶ σοῦ γε κατθανεῖν ὑφειμένην .
sections.translation: I know she did submit to die in thy stead .

sections.label: Section 525
sections.original: πῶς οὖν ἔτ ’ ἔστιν , εἴπερ ᾔνεσεν τάδε ;
sections.translation: How then is she still alive , if so she promised ?

sections.label: Section 526
sections.original: ἆ , μὴ πρόκλαι ’ ἄκοιτιν , ἐς τότ ’ ἀμβαλοῦ .
sections.translation: Ah ! weep not thy wife before the day , put that off till then .

sections.label: Section 527
sections.original: τέθνηχ ’ ὁ μέλλων , κοὐκέτ ’ ἔσθ ’ ὁ κατθανών .
sections.translation: The doomed is dead ; the dead no more exists .

sections.label: Section 528
sections.original: χωρὶς τό τ ’ εἶναι καὶ τὸ μὴ νομίζεται .
sections.translation: Men count to be and not to be something apart .

sections.label: Section 529
sections.original: σὺ τῇδε κρίνεις , Ἡράκλεις , κείνῃ δ ’ ἐγώ .
sections.translation: Thy verdict this , O Heracles , mine another .

sections.label: Section 530
sections.original: τί δῆτα κλαίεις ; τίς φίλων ὁ κατθανών ;
sections.translation: Why weepest then ? which of thy dear ones is the dead ?

sections.label: Section 531
sections.original: γυνή · γυναικὸς ἀρτίως μεμνήμεθα .
sections.translation: ’ Tis a woman ; I spoke of a woman just now .

sections.label: Section 532
sections.original: ὀθνεῖος ἢ σοὶ συγγενὴς γεγῶσά τις ;
sections.translation: A stranger , or one of thine own kin ?

sections.label: Section 533
sections.original: ὀθνεῖος , ἄλλως δ ’ ἦν ἀναγκαία δόμοις .
sections.translation: A stranger , yet in another sense related to my house .

sections.label: Section 534
sections.original: πῶς οὖν ἐν οἴκοις σοῖσιν ὤλεσεν βίον ;
sections.translation: How then came she by her death in house of thine ?

sections.label: Section 535
sections.original: πατρὸς θανόντος ἐνθάδ ’ ὠρφανεύετο .
sections.translation: Her father dead , she lived here as an orphan .

sections.label: Section 536
sections.original: φεῦ .
sections.translation: Ah ! would I had found thee free from grief , Admetus !

sections.label: Section 537
sections.original: ὡς δὴ τί δράσων τόνδ ’ ὑπορράπτεις λόγον ;
sections.translation: With what intent dost thou devise this speech ?

sections.label: Section 538
sections.original: ξένων πρὸς ἄλλων ἑστίαν πορεύσομαι .
sections.translation: I will seek some other friendly hearth .
